title: gavilea platyantha
intro: Gavilea platyantha, also known as the 'gavilea' or 'yellow gavilea', is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other compounds that provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help boost the immune system and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, Gavilea platyantha is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
